22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)

86	class SamplerOutput(
87	        msgspec.Struct,
88	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
89	        array_like=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
90	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
91	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
92	
93	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
94	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
95	    """
96	
97	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]
98	
99	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.
100	    sampled_token_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
101	
102	    # On-device tensor containing the logprobs of each token.
103	    logprobs: Optional["torch.Tensor"] = None
104	
105	    # Holds either (1) the pythonized sampler result (single-step scheduling)
106	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the
107	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)
108	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.
111	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
114	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
116	
117	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
118	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are
119	    # specified in lieu of prompt token ids or text.
120	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
121	
122	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.
123	    spec_decode_worker_metrics: Optional[SpecDecodeWorkerMetrics] = None
124	
125	    # Optional last hidden states from the model.
126	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
127	
128	    # Optional prefill hidden states from the model
129	    # (used for models like EAGLE).
130	    prefill_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
131	
132	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers
133	    model_forward_time: Optional[float] = None
134	
135	    # Time taken in the model execute function. This will include model forward,
136	    # block/sync across workers, cpu-gpu sync time and sampling time.
137	    model_execute_time: Optional[float] = None
138	
139	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
140	        return self.outputs[idx]
141	
142	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):
143	        self.outputs[idx] = value
144	
145	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]:
146	        return iter(self.outputs)
147	
148	    def __len__(self):
149	        return len(self.outputs)

400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []

402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435

437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get

440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None

443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:

453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens

459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442	
443	
444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results
477	
478	
479	def _random_sample(
480	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
481	    random_samples: torch.Tensor,
482	) -> SampleResultType:
483	    """Run random sampling on a given samples.
484	
485	    Args:
486	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
487	        random_samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The
488	            length of samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
489	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
490	    Returns:
491	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
492	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
493	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
494	    """
495	    # Find the maximum n value of the prompt phase requests.
496	    random_samples = random_samples.cpu()
497	    sample_idx = 0
498	    results: SampleResultType = []
499	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
500	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
501	            results.append(([], []))
502	            continue
503	
504	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
505	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
506	        is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
507	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
508	        if is_prompt:
509	            # Prompt phase.
510	            parent_ids = [0] * sampling_params.n
511	            next_token_ids = random_samples[
512	                sample_idx, :sampling_params.n].tolist()
513	        else:
514	            # Generation phase.
515	            parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
516	            next_token_ids = random_samples[sample_idx:sample_idx +
517	                                            num_parent_seqs, 0].tolist()
518	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
519	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
520	    return results
521	
522	
523	# torch.multinomial forces a GPU<->CPU sync.
524	# Therefore, we use an optimized implementation instead.
525	# Note that we always sample with replacement.
526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)
548	
549	
550	def _top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer(
551	        probs: torch.Tensor, top_ks: torch.Tensor, top_ps: torch.Tensor,
552	        num_samples: int, seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]]):
553	    if num_samples > 1:
554	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
555	        top_ks = top_ks.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
556	        top_ps = top_ps.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
557	    batch_next_token_ids = flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling(
558	        probs,
559	        top_ks,
560	        top_ps,
561	    )
562	    return batch_next_token_ids.view(-1, num_samples)
563	
564	
565	def get_pythonized_sample_results(
566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''
582	
583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue
600	        (seq_group_id, seq_groups) = sample_metadata[sampling_type]
601	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
602	            sample_results = _greedy_sample(seq_groups, greedy_samples)
603	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
604	            sample_results = _random_sample(seq_groups,
605	                                            multinomial_samples[sampling_type])
606	        sample_results_dict.update(zip(seq_group_id, sample_results))
607	
608	    return [
609	        sample_results_dict.get(i, ([], []))
610	        for i in range(len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups))
611	    ]
612	
613	
614	def _sample_with_torch(
615	    probs: torch.Tensor,
616	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
617	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
618	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
619	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
620	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
621	) -> SampleReturnType:
622	    '''Torch-oriented _sample() implementation.
623	
624	    Single-step scheduling:
625	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
626	    * Immediately Pythonize sampling result
627	
628	    Multi-step scheduling:
629	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
630	    * Defer Pythonization & preserve GPU-side
631	      tensors required for Pythonization
632	    '''
633	
634	    categorized_seq_group_ids: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
635	        t: []
636	        for t in SamplingType
637	    }
638	    categorized_sample_indices = sampling_metadata.categorized_sample_indices
639	    for i, seq_group in enumerate(sampling_metadata.seq_groups):
640	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
641	        sampling_type = sampling_params.sampling_type
642	        categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type].append(i)
643	
644	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType = {}
645	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType = {}
646	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType = {}
647	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
14	
15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
16	
17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \

90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
601	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs

610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:
613	            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)

624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0

626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:

639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],

680	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
681	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
682	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
683	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
684	            device="cpu",
685	            dtype=torch.int64,
686	            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
687	        )
688	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
689	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
690	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
691	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
692	        # token_id of this value.
693	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
694	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
695	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
696	                                              non_blocking=True)

698	    def make_lora_inputs(
699	        self, num_scheduled_tokens: np.ndarray
700	    ) -> tuple[tuple[int, ...], tuple[int, ...], set[LoRARequest]]:
701	        """
702	        Given the num_scheduled_tokens for each request in the batch, return
703	        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
704	        Returns:
705	            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
706	               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
707	            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
708	               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.
709	            3. lora_requests: Set of relevant LoRA requests.
710	        """
711	
712	        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]
713	        prompt_lora_mapping = tuple(req_lora_mapping)
714	        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
715	            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
716	        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
717	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
718	
719	        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests

388	        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().

390	        Args:

404	        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)

489	    def condense(self) -> None:
490	        """Slide non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
491	
492	        Any consecutive empty indices at the very end of the list are not
493	        filled.
494	
495	        Args:
496	          empty_req_indices: empty indices which may be filled.
497	
498	        Returns:
499	          swaps: list of (from,to) swap tuples for moved requests
500	          empty_req_indices: indices not filled by condensation
501	        """
502	        if not (empty_req_indices := self.batch_update_builder.removed):
503	            # All removed requests were replaced by added requests, or else no
504	            # requests were removed at all. No condense() needed
505	            return
506	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
507	        if num_reqs == 0:
508	            # The batched states are empty.
509	            self._req_ids.clear()
510	            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
511	            return
512	
513	        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
514	        # is sorted in descending order.
515	        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
516	        while empty_req_indices:
517	            # Find the largest non-empty index.
518	            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
519	                last_req_index -= 1
520	
521	            # Find the smallest empty index.
522	            empty_index = self.batch_update_builder.peek_removed()
523	            assert empty_index is not None
524	            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
525	                break
526	
527	            # Move active request down into empty request
528	            # index.
529	            self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()
530	            self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
531	                (last_req_index, empty_index,
532	                 MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL))
533	            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
534	            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
535	            assert req_id is not None
536	            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
537	            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
538	            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
539	            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
540	            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index
541	
542	            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
543	            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
544	                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
545	            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
546	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
547	                last_req_index]
548	            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
549	                last_req_index]
550	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
551	                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
552	            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
553	            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
554	                last_req_index]
555	            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
556	            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
557	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
558	                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
559	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
560	                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
561	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
562	                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
563	            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
564	            if generator is not None:
565	                self.generators[empty_index] = generator
566	
567	            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
568	                last_req_index]
569	
570	            # TODO convert these to LogitsProcessors
571	            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
572	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
573	                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
574	                        last_req_index]
575	
576	            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
577	                last_req_index, None)
578	            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
579	                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids
580	
581	            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
582	            last_req_index -= 1
583	
584	        # Trim lists to the batch size.
585	        del self._req_ids[self.num_reqs:]
586	        del self.req_output_token_ids[self.num_reqs:]

588	    def refresh_metadata(self):
589	        """Apply batch updates, reset input batch at end of step
590	        
591	        * Apply batch add/remove/permute to logits procs' states
592	        * If batch state is modified, update sampling metadata
593	        """
594	        batch_update = self.batch_update_builder.get_and_reset(self.num_reqs)
595	        for logit_proc in self.logitsprocs.all:
596	            logit_proc.update_state(batch_update)
597	        if batch_update:
598	            self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:

604	                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)

610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:

614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
621	                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)
622	
623	        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
625	                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
627	            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
628	            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
629	            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
630	            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
631	            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
632	        else:
633	            prompt_token_ids = None
634	
635	        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
636	        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
637	            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
638	            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
647	            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
648	            generators=self.generators,

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Summary of vLLM Sampler Implementation

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase. Here's the detailed breakdown of the sampling implementation:

### 1. **Main Sampling Logic Files**

**Primary sampler file:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 169-335)
  - Main `Sampler` class orchestrates the entire sampling pipeline
  - Applies penalties → temperature scaling → top_k/top_p filtering → softmax → sampling

**V1 version (newer API):**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (lines 18-127)
  - Uses `TopKTopPSampler` module for filtering
  - Simpler, cleaner flow with explicit temperature and filtering steps

**Top-k/Top-p implementation:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` (lines 22-234)
  - Main filtering implementation (both native PyTorch and FlashInfer backed)

---

### 2. **The Sampling Pipeline Flow**

**Order of operations (from logits → sampled token):**

1. **Apply min_tokens penalty** → set stop tokens to `-inf` if min_tokens not reached (lines 269, 351-395)
2. **Apply presence/frequency/repetition penalties** → modify logits (lines 271-277)
3. **Temperature scaling** → divide logits by temperature (lines 281-282)
4. **Top-k filtering** → keep only top-k tokens (applied first when both top-k and top-p used)
5. **Top-p filtering** → keep tokens whose cumulative probability ≤ p (applied second)
6. **Min-p filtering** (optional) → remove tokens below p * max_prob (lines 288-289)
7. **Softmax** → convert filtered logits to probabilities (line 293)
8. **Sample** → multinomial sampling from probabilities (lines 298-305)

---

### 3. **Top-k Filtering Implementation**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 211-233

```python
def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    
    # Get the threshold value: the k-th largest logit
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    
    # Set all logits below threshold to -inf
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Key points:**
- Uses `torch.topk()` to find k-th largest value
- Sets all values **below** the k-th largest to `-inf` 
- Efficient: doesn't sort entire vocab, only needs topk operation
- Optimized for top-k only case (avoids full sort)

---

### 4. **Top-p Filtering Implementation**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 168-208

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    
    # Step 1: Sort logits in ascending order (ascending!)
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Step 2: Apply top-k if present
    if k is not None:
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 3: Apply top-p
    if p is not None:
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)           # Convert to probs
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)       # Cumulative sum
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)   # Find tokens to remove
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False                          # Keep at least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 4: Scatter back to original order
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Top-p Logic Breakdown:**
- **Sort:** Sorts logits in **ascending order** (smallest to largest)
- **Compute softmax:** Converts sorted logits to sorted probabilities
- **Cumulative sum:** Accumulates probabilities from smallest to largest
- **Filter:** Keeps tokens where `cumsum ≤ 1 - p` (removes lowest probability tokens until cumsum > 1 - p)
- **At least one token:** `top_p_mask[:, -1] = False` ensures the largest token is always kept
- **Scatter back:** Restores tokens to original vocabulary order

**Why ascending sort?** Cumulative sum from smallest allows efficient filtering: if you traverse from lowest probability upward, once you exceed the threshold, all earlier tokens are removed.

---

### 5. **Data Structures**

**Main sampling tensors (SamplingTensors):**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` lines 372-384

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor              # Shape: (num_seqs,)
    top_ps: torch.Tensor                   # Shape: (num_seqs,)
    top_ks: torch.Tensor                   # Shape: (num_seqs,)
    min_ps: torch.Tensor                   # Shape: (num_seqs,)
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor        # Shape: (num_seqs,)
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor       # Shape: (num_seqs,)
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor      # Shape: (num_seqs,)
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor             # For penalty calculation
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor             # For penalty calculation
```

**Logits tensor:**
- Shape: `(num_tokens, vocab_size)` where `num_tokens = batch_size` in generation phase
- Each row represents logits for one token position
- Modified **in-place** by most filtering operations to avoid memory copies

**Probabilities tensor (after softmax):**
- Shape: `(num_tokens, vocab_size)`
- Used for final multinomial sampling
- Computed with `torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)` (lines 293)

---

### 6. **Filter Application Order (Critical)**

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 269-295:

```
1. min_tokens_penalty (lines 269)
2. Presence/Frequency/Repetition penalties (lines 272-277)
3. Temperature scaling (lines 281-282)
4. Top-k filtering (lines 284-286) ← BEFORE top-p when both present
5. Top-p filtering (lines 284-286) ← AFTER top-k
6. Min-p filtering (lines 288-289)
7. Softmax to probabilities (line 293)
8. Multinomial sampling (lines 298-305)
```

**Important:** When **both top-k and top-p** are applied:
- Top-k is applied **first** in the sorted array
- Top-p is applied **second** on the already-filtered results
- This order is evident in lines 189-204 of `topk_topp_sampler.py`

---

### 7. **Sampling Methods**

**Three sampling types:**

1. **Greedy Sampling** - `_greedy_sample()` (lines 444-476 in sampler.py)
   - Simply takes `argmax(logits)`
   - No filtering needed
   - Deterministic

2. **Random Sampling** - `_random_sample()` (lines 479-520 in sampler.py)
   - Multinomial sampling: `sample ~ Categorical(probs)`
   - Uses exponential sampling trick to avoid PyTorch multinomial (which causes CPU-GPU sync)
   - Formula: `sample = argmax(probs / exponential_random_vars)`

3. **Random Seed Sampling** - Same as random but with per-request generators

---

### 8. **Performance Optimizations**

**Key optimizations in the code:**

1. **Exponential sampling trick** (lines 528-547):
   - Avoids `torch.multinomial()` which causes CPU-GPU synchronization
   - Samples using Gumbel-max trick: `argmax(log(probs) - log(-log(uniform)))`
   - Implemented as: `argmax(probs / exponential())`

2. **In-place operations:**
   - Most filtering operations modify logits in-place to avoid memory copies
   - Comments note this: "The logits tensor may be updated in-place"

3. **FlashInfer backend** (optional):
   - If available, uses `flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits()` for fused operation
   - Falls back to native PyTorch if not available
   - Can be disabled with `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=0`

4. **Top-k without top-p optimization**:
   - `apply_top_k_only()` avoids full sort, uses only `topk()` operation (lines 211-233)
   - Faster than full sort when only top-k is needed

---

### 9. **Test Files for Understanding**

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py` - Shows top-k/top-p filtering tests
  - Line 48: Tests that top-k only matches top-p with p=1.0
  - Lines 97-113: Compares PyTorch and FlashInfer implementations

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Main file** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (V0) or `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (V1) |
| **Top-k/p logic** | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` |
| **Filter order** | Penalties → Temp → Top-k → Top-p → Min-p → Softmax → Sample |
| **Top-k method** | Uses `torch.topk()` to find threshold, sets below-threshold to -inf |
| **Top-p method** | Sort ascending → softmax → cumsum → mask where cumsum ≤ 1-p → scatter back |
| **Sampling method** | Exponential trick: `argmax(probs / exponential_random())` |
| **Logits shape** | `(num_tokens, vocab_size)` |
| **In-place ops** | Yes, logits modified in-place to save memory |

| **Main file** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (V0) or `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (V1) |
| **Top-k/p logic** | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` |
| **Filter order** | Penalties → Temp → Top-k → Top-p → Min-p → Softmax → Sample |
| **Top-k method** | Uses `torch.topk()` to find threshold, sets below-threshold to -inf |
| **Top-p method** | Sort ascending → softmax → cumsum → mask where cumsum ≤ 1-p → scatter back |
| **Sampling method** | Exponential trick: `argmax(probs / exponential_random())` |
| **Logits shape** | `(num_tokens, vocab_size)` |
| **In-place ops** | Yes, logits modified in-place to save memory |

Perfect! I now have comprehensive information about the sampling parameters system. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive Report: vLLM Sampling Parameters Architecture

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and mapped out how sampling parameters are defined, validated, and used. Here are my findings:

---

### 1. SamplingParams Definition & Fields

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Class Definition:** Lines 118-603 (msgspec.Struct)

**Key sampling fields:**
- **Line 212:** `top_p: float = 1.0` (cumulative probability threshold)
- **Line 213:** `top_k: int = 0` (number of top tokens to consider)
- **Line 211:** `temperature: float = 1.0` (randomness control)
- **Line 210:** `repetition_penalty: float = 1.0`
- **Line 209:** `frequency_penalty: float = 0.0`
- **Line 208:** `presence_penalty: float = 0.0`
- **Line 214:** `min_p: float = 0.0` (minimum probability threshold)

---

### 2. Validation Logic for Sampling Parameters

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Validation Method:** Lines 387-462 (`_verify_args()` called in `__post_init__`)

**Key validations for top_p and top_k:**
- **Line 417-418:** `top_p` must be in range `(0, 1]` 
  ```python
  if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
      raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
  ```

- **Lines 420-425:** `top_k` must be `0` (disabled), `-1` (also disabled), or >= 1, must be int type:
  ```python
  if self.top_k < -1:
      raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, got {self.top_k}.")
  if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
      raise TypeError(f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
  ```

- **Lines 377-382:** When temperature < 1e-5 (greedy sampling), top_p/top_k are overridden:
  ```python
  if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
      self.top_p = 1.0
      self.top_k = 0
      self.min_p = 0.0
  ```

---

### 3. Data Transfer Objects (Metadata & Tensors)

#### A. v0 (Standard) Sampling Metadata

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**SequenceGroupToSample dataclass:** Lines 20-62
- Holds `sampling_params: SamplingParams` (line 30)
- Contains `seq_ids`, `seq_data`, `generator`, `is_prompt`, indices for sampling

**SamplingMetadata class:** Lines 97-196
- **Line 141:** `seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]` - contains sampling params
- **Line 142:** `selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor` - indices to extract logits
- **Line 143:** `categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]` - organized by sampling type
- **Static Method `prepare()`:** Lines 149-188 - converts seq_group_metadata to SamplingMetadata

**SamplingTensors dataclass:** Lines 372-597 (holds batched parameters for GPU)
- **Lines 376-378:** `top_ps: torch.Tensor`, `top_ks: torch.Tensor` (extracted from sampling_params)
- **Lines 377, 379-382:** Temperature, presence/frequency/repetition penalties as tensors
- **Lines 394-495:** `from_sampling_metadata()` - extracts top_p/top_k from each sampling_params:

```python
# Line 415-420:
for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
    top_p = sampling_params.top_p
    top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
    top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
```

#### B. v1 (New) Sampling Metadata

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478830004-ou7zva/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

**SamplingMetadata dataclass:** Lines 12-44
- **Line 19:** `top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]` - batched top_p values
- **Line 20:** `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]` - batched top_k values
- **Line 15:** `temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]`
- Other fields: `generators`, `frequency_penalties`, `presence_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`

---

### 4. Parameter Flow: SamplingParams → Intermediate Structures → Sampler

#### **Path in v0 (Standard):**
1. **Input:** `SamplingParams` with `top_p=0.9`, `top_k=50`
2. **Step 1:** `SequenceGroupToSample` created in `_prepare_seq_groups()` (sampling_metadata.py:198-369)
3. **Step 2:** `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` (sampling_metadata.py:387-495) extracts:
   - Collects `top_p` values from each seq_group → creates `top_ps: torch.Tensor`
   - Collects `top_k` values from each seq_group → creates `top_ks: torch.Tensor`
4. **Step 3:** Sampler uses these tensors (sampler.py:225-335):
   - Line 285-286: `_apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps, sampling_tensors.top_ks)`
   - Line 398-424: Implementation applies masks to logits based on top_p/top_k

#### **Path in v1 (GPU Input Batch):**
1. **Input:** `CachedRequestState` with `sampling_params: SamplingParams`
2. **Step 1:** `InputBatch.add_request()` (gpu_input_batch.py:266-385) extracts sampling params:
   - **Line 312:** `self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p`
   - **Line 315-320:** `top_k` extracted with validation and clamping:
     ```python
     top_k = sampling_params.top_k
     if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
         self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
     else:
         top_k = self.vocab_size
     self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
     ```
3. **Step 2:** `_make_sampling_metadata()` (gpu_input_batch.py:600-659) creates v1 SamplingMetadata:
   - **Line 646:** `top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs]`
   - **Line 647:** `top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs]`
4. **Step 3:** Sampler.sample() (v1/sample/sampler.py:90-137) uses TopKTopPSampler:
   - **Line 121-126:** Calls `self.topk_topp_sampler(logits, generators, top_k, top_p)`

---

### 5. Actual Sampling Implementations

#### v0 Sampler (sampler.py:398-424)
**Function:** `_apply_top_k_top_p()`
- Takes tensors of top_p and top_k values per sequence
- Applies top-k filtering: keeps only k highest probability tokens
- Applies top-p filtering: keeps tokens until cumulative probability reaches p
- Returns modified logits with -inf masking

#### v1 TopKTopPSampler (v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py:22-119)
**Multiple implementations:**
- **Line 69-83:** `forward_native()` - PyTorch-native implementation
- **Line 85-107:** `forward_cuda()` - Uses FlashInfer library for GPU optimization
- **Line 109-118:** `forward_tpu()` - TPU-specific implementation
- All call `apply_top_k_top_p()` (line 168-210) for filtering

---

### 6. Key Type Information

| Parameter | Type (SamplingParams) | Storage (SamplingTensors/v1) | Range/Validation |
|-----------|----------------------|------------------------------|------------------|
| `top_p` | `float` | `torch.Tensor(dtype=float32)` | Must be in `(0, 1]` |
| `top_k` | `int` | `torch.Tensor(dtype=int32)` | `0`, `-1` (disabled), or `>= 1` |
| `temperature` | `float` | `torch.Tensor(dtype=float32)` | `>= 0` |
| `freq_penalty` | `float` | `torch.Tensor(dtype=float)` | `[-2, 2]` |
| `presence_penalty` | `float` | `torch.Tensor(dtype=float)` | `[-2, 2]` |
| `repetition_penalty` | `float` | `torch.Tensor(dtype=float)` | `> 0` |

---

### 7. Summary of File Locations

| Responsibility | File Path | Key Lines |
|---|---|---|
| **SamplingParams Definition** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 118-603 |
| **top_p Validation** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 417-418 |
| **top_k Validation** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 420-425 |
| **Greedy Sampling Override** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 377-382 |
| **v0 Metadata Creation** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 149-188, 387-495 |
| **v0 SamplingTensors** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 372-597 |
| **v0 Sampler** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 225-335 |
| **v0 Top-K/P Implementation** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 398-424 |
| **v1 Metadata** | `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | 12-44 |
| **v1 InputBatch Processing** | `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` | 266-385, 600-659 |
| **v1 Sampler** | `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | 18-137 |
| **v1 TopK/P Sampler** | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 22-210 |

---

### 8. Data Flow Diagram (Simplified)

```
SamplingParams (top_p=0.9, top_k=50)
    ↓
[v0] SequenceGroupToSample (wraps SamplingParams)
    ↓
[v0] SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    ↓
[v0] top_ps, top_ks: torch.Tensor
    ↓
[v0] Sampler._apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)
    ↓
Logits with -inf masking → Softmax → Sample

---

SamplingParams (top_p=0.9, top_k=50)
    ↓
[v1] InputBatch.add_request()
    ↓
[v1] top_p_cpu[idx], top_k_cpu[idx] = scalar values
    ↓
[v1] _make_sampling_metadata() → SamplingMetadata
    ↓
[v1] top_p, top_k: torch.Tensor (batched)
    ↓
[v1] Sampler.sample() → TopKTopPSampler
    ↓
Logits → apply_top_k_top_p() → Probs → Sample
```

This comprehensive mapping shows how sampling parameters flow from user input through validation, into batched tensor structures, and finally into the actual sampling kernels.

109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2
116	
117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.

410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:

class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
    CUMULATIVE = 0
    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
    DELTA = 1
    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
    FINAL_ONLY = 2


class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
        min_tokens: int = 0,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        detokenize: bool = True,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> "SamplingParams":
        if logit_bias is not None:
            # Convert token_id to integer
            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
            logit_bias = {
                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
            }

        return SamplingParams(
            n=1 if n is None else n,
            best_of=best_of,
            presence_penalty=0.0
            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
            frequency_penalty=0.0
            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
            repetition_penalty=1.0
            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            seed=seed,
            stop=stop,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            bad_words=bad_words,
            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            min_tokens=min_tokens,
            logprobs=logprobs,
            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
            detokenize=detokenize,
            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
            logits_processors=logits_processors,
            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
            output_kind=output_kind,
            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
            logit_bias=logit_bias,
            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
            extra_args=extra_args,
        )

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        # how we deal with `best_of``:
        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
        # when we return the result, we will check
        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
        if self.best_of:
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
            if not self._real_n:
                self._real_n = self.n
                self.n = self.best_of

        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
            logger.warning(
                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)

        if self.seed == -1:
            self.seed = None

        if self.stop is None:
            self.stop = []
        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
            self.stop = [self.stop]

        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
            self.stop_token_ids = []

        if self.bad_words is None:
            self.bad_words = []

        if self.logprobs is True:
            self.logprobs = 1

        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
            self.prompt_logprobs = 1

        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
        # until sequence is finished.
        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1

        self._verify_args()

        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
            self.top_p = 1.0
            self.top_k = 0
            self.min_p = 0.0
            self._verify_greedy_sampling()

        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)

    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
        if self.n < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
        if self.best_of is not None:
            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
            if self.best_of < 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
        if self.top_k < -1:
            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
            raise TypeError(
                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                             f"{self.min_p}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
        if self.min_tokens < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
            raise ValueError(
                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")

    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
        if self.n > 1:
            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
                             f"got {self.n}.")

    def update_from_generation_config(
            self,
            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""

        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
            # min_tokens processing.
            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)

        # Update eos_token_id for generation
        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
            # it can be either int or list of int
            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
                # purposes.
                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
            if eos_ids:
                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
                if not self.ignore_eos:
                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)

    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
